title: Is the zero-divisor graph Γ(Z_97 × Z_25) prime?
generalization_signal: The verified z97 feeder supports a genuine finite-range complementary-support template on the four-class Γ(Z_p × Z_25) line: the fixed data A = A0, B = {1,293,307,311}, and C0 = {2 <= n <= 291 : gcd(n,6) = 1} already supply the exact endpoint p = 97 and indicate a plausible 59 <= p <= 97 slice, while showing that p = 101 is the first place where the present C-supply mechanism must change.
strongest_honest_claim: An explicit prime-labeling witness for Γ(Z_97 × Z_25) survives skeptical verification, and the same fixed complementary-support reservoir and zero-spill barrier set appear to support a real finite-range theorem slice through 59 <= p <= 97. This is strong feeder evidence for the active family campaign, but it remains an unformalized instance-plus-slice candidate rather than an exact Lean-backed theorem or paper-ready family result.
candidate_theorem_slice: Finite-range F25 complementary-support slice: for odd primes 59 <= p <= 97, let A = A0, B = {1,293,307,311}, choose any (p - 1)-subset C of {2 <= n <= 291 : gcd(n,6) = 1}, and let D be the residual complement in {1,...,5p+19}; then the ring-to-support reduction and the classwise coprimality argument yield a prime labeling of Γ(Z_p × Z_25).
next_action: Promote the verified z97 feeder into the family generalize/publication package as endpoint evidence for the finite-range 59 <= p <= 97 complementary-support slice, then test the first real boundary shift at z101-z25 or formalize the finite-range claim if the bridge lemmas are ready.
